items.docs: Returns the admin interface home page with links to all available endpoints.
Returns a list of all available admin endpoints and their descriptions.
Returns a 200 OK response if the server has been initialized and is ready to accept traffic. Returns 503 if the server is not yet initialized.
Causes the server to fail inbound health checks. This requires the use of the HTTP health check filter. Useful for draining a server prior to shutting it down or performing a full restart.
Negates the effect of POST /healthcheck/fail, causing the server to resume passing inbound health checks.
Returns information about the running Envoy server including version, uptime, command line options, and hot restart version.
Returns current memory allocation and heap usage in bytes, providing insights into memory consumption of the Envoy process.

info.name: Envoy Proxy Get Hot Restart Version

items.docs: Returns the hot restart compatibility version. This can be matched against the output of the --hot-restart-version command line option to determine whether a new binary and the running binary are hot restart compatible.
Cleanly exits the Envoy server process.

params.name: format
params.description: Output format for the statistics.

params.name: filter
params.description: Regular expression to filter the returned statistics by name.

params.name: usedonly
params.description: Only return statistics that have been written to by Envoy.

params.name: histogram_buckets
params.description: Control histogram output mode.

items.docs: Returns statistics that Envoy has collected, including counters, gauges, and histograms. Stats are provided as key-value pairs using a hierarchical dotted notation.

info.name: Envoy Proxy Get Statistics in Prometheus Format

params.name: usedonly
params.description: Only return statistics that have been written to by Envoy.

params.name: text_readouts
params.description: Include text readout gauges in the output.

params.name: filter
params.description: Regular expression to filter the returned statistics by name.

items.docs: Returns statistics in Prometheus exposition format suitable for scraping by a Prometheus server.

info.name: Envoy Proxy Get Recent Stat Lookups

items.docs: Returns a table of recently looked-up stat names.
Resets all counters to zero. This is useful when used in conjunction with GET /stats during debugging sessions.

params.name: format
params.description: Output format for cluster information.

items.docs: Lists all configured cluster manager clusters including all discovered upstream hosts in each cluster along with per-host statistics. Provides information about the health status and connection metrics of upstream hosts.

params.name: resource
params.description: Filter the configuration dump to only return the specified resource type.

params.name: mask
params.description: Field mask to apply to the configuration dump, limiting which fields are returned.

params.name: name_regex
params.description: Regular expression to filter the returned configuration by resource name.

params.name: include_eds
params.description: Include EDS configuration in the dump.

items.docs: Returns the currently loaded configuration in JSON format, including bootstrap, clusters, endpoints, listeners, scoped routes, routes, secrets, and ECDS filter configurations.

params.name: mask
params.description: Field mask to apply to the initialization dump.

items.docs: Returns the unmodified configuration as it was loaded during the initialization process.

params.name: format
params.description: Output format for listener information.

items.docs: Lists all configured listeners, including their names and the addresses they are listening on.

params.name: graceful
params.description: When set, listeners are drained gracefully with a period where both old and new listeners accept connections.

params.name: inboundonly
params.description: Drain only inbound listeners and leave outbound listeners intact.

items.docs: Drains all listeners, causing them to stop accepting new connections. Existing connections are allowed to complete.
Lists all loaded TLS certificates including file name, serial number, subject alternate names, and days until expiration in JSON format.
Returns the current runtime settings, including any overrides that have been applied via the admin interface or runtime layer configuration.

params.name: key
params.description: Key-value pairs to set as runtime overrides. Multiple parameters can be passed.

items.docs: Adds or modifies runtime values as passed in query parameters. To delete a previously added override, use an empty value for the parameter.

info.name: Envoy Proxy View or Modify Log Levels

params.name: level
params.description: Set the log level for all loggers. Valid values include trace, debug, info, warning/warn, error, critical, and off.

params.name: paths
params.description: Set a glob pattern for file path to filter the log output.

items.docs: Enable or change logging levels at runtime. Without query parameters, returns the current logging levels. With a query parameter, sets the logging level for a specific logger or all loggers.

info.name: Envoy Proxy Enable or Disable CPU Profiler

params.name: enable
params.description: Enable (y) or disable (n) the CPU profiler.

items.docs: Enables or disables the CPU profiler. Requires compilation with gperftools. The output file can be then analyzed with pprof.

info.name: Envoy Proxy Enable or Disable Heap Profiler

params.name: enable
params.description: Enable (y) or disable (n) the heap profiler.

items.docs: Enables or disables the heap profiler. Requires compilation with gperftools.
Installs a traffic tap configuration that allows inspecting traffic matching specific filter rules. The tap configuration is supplied in the request body.

info.name: Envoy Proxy Get Mutex Contention Stats

items.docs: Dumps current Envoy mutex contention stats in JSON format, if mutex tracing is enabled.
